message:
fix(indexer): add fsync before atomic-rename in save_watch_state and heartbeat lock

save_watch_state wrote temp file via fs::write (no fsync) before
rename — a crash between rename returning and data reaching disk left
watch_state.json with zeroed/partial data, losing per-connector
last_message_id_ms cursors and forcing a full re-walk on next start.

heartbeat_index_run_lock did an in-place fs::write (O_TRUNC) with no
atomicity at all — a crash mid-write left the lock file truncated,
potentially poisoning heartbeat parsing and causing stale-lock
mis-detection under concurrent indexer contention.

Both now use File::create + BufWriter + sync_all + replace_file_from_temp,
matching the proven pattern in write_json_pretty_atomically.

[c5sgs] [39squ]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
